items.title: Residents urged to stay away from southern Lebanon as Israeli strikes continue
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanon's army warned people against returning to the country's south on Wednesday, where the Israeli military is still launching attacks, as Israel said the ceasefire with Iran did not include its conflict with Hizbollah. "In light of regional developments and reports circulating about a ceasefire, (the army) urges citizens to wait before returning to southern villages and towns and to avoid approaching areas where Israeli occupation forces have advanced... since they may be exposing themselves to the ongoing Israeli attacks," the Lebanese army said in a statement. An AFP correspondent in the Tyre region saw a small number of people heading south, from where hundreds of thousands have been displaced since Lebanon was drawn into the war on March 2.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Man cultivated IS ideology in family, court told
items.summary: Deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Mustaffa P. Kunyalam said Radin Imran Radin Mohd Yassin, 64, the first accused, had systematically isolated his family from the local community while instilling violent ideological teachings linked to IS. The prosecutor said the indoctrination took place at Radin Imran's family home in Kampung Sungai Tiram from the end of 2014 until May 17, 2024, when police arrested five family members following the deadly attack. The accused comprise Radin Imran; his Singaporean wife, Rosna Jantan; 61, and their children — Radin Romyullah, 36, Mariah, 21, and Farhah Sobrina, 25.

items.title: Kelantan takes serious view of mat rempit activities at LTSIP
items.summary: KOTA BHARU: The Kelantan state government takes a serious view of the issue of unhealthy activities involving a small group of youths in the vicinity of the Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP), Pengkalan Chepa, which recently went viral on social media. State Youth, Sports, NGO and Community Unity Committee chairman Zamakhshari Muhamad said that there should be no compromise on actions that could endanger public safety, and such actions must be addressed firmly through enforcement actions by the authorities. Zamakhshari said the state government will adopt a comprehensive approach encompassing enforcement aspects, youth development, recreational facility provision, and alternative activities to address the issue.

items.title: Iranian drone strike hits Bahrain hours after ceasefire announced
items.summary: MANAMA: Iranian attacks on Bahrain injured two people, the Gulf kingdom's interior ministry said on Wednesday, hours after the United States announced a ceasefire with Iran. Shortly after the ceasefire was announced, Qatar's defence ministry also announced it was dealing with a missile threat. Before the truce was publicised, Doha's interior ministry said falling debris from an earlier intercepted missile salvo had injured four people, including one child.

items.title: SWCorp: Cigarette butts remain top contributor to littering offences
items.summary: MELAKA, April 8 — Littering cigarette butts remains the most common offence under the Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Act 2007, accounting for over 1,700 of the 2,154 notices issued as of April 7. Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) chief executive officer Khalid Mohamed said the figures reflect low awareness among smokers on public cleanliness. “Out of the total number of cases, the offence of littering of cigarette butts continues to record the highest number, indicating that a careless attitude towards public cleanliness still persists.

items.title: Visit Malaysia Year 2026 extended until end of next year
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Cabinet today was informed of the extension of Visit Malaysia Year 2026 (VM2026) until the end of 2027 by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, the Communications Ministry said. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said that Tourism Minister Tiong King Sing had also informed the government that there is a need to examine the aviation and hospitality sectors. "This is to ensure they (tourism sector) are not affected too heavily by the global energy crisis and the conflicts in the Middle East," he said in Putrajaya today.

items.title: Acting US attorney general defends probes of Trump foes
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Acting US Attorney General Todd Blanche on Tuesday defended investigations of individuals Donald Trump "has had issues with" and said only the president knows why his predecessor, Pam Bondi, was fired. Blanche, Trump's former personal lawyer, said only the president knows why Bondi was dismissed. "Nobody has any idea why the attorney general is no longer the attorney general and I'm the acting attorney general, except for President Trump," he said.

items.title: Cabinet mulls increased security at petrol stations as diesel, petrol smuggling attempts rise
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — The Cabinet is considering increasing security presence at petrol stations following several attempts to smuggle or hoard petrol and diesel amid escalating prices, Communication Minister Fahmi Fadzil said today. “Because of the series of recent arrests made, the Cabinet is thinking of increasing security at petrol stations, especially those near the borders,” he said at his weekly post-Cabinet press conference. “This may include deploying PDRM officers to ensure the aspect of security at these petrol stations are safeguarded.” MORE TO COME

items.title: Israel renews strikes on south Lebanon despite Mideast truce
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel renewed its strikes in south Lebanon today, as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu insisted the Iran war truce does not include Lebanon. The strikes came despite Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, which drew Lebanon into the Middle East war by attacking Israel on March 2, not claiming any operations since 1am (Tuesday 2200 GMT). Israel renewed an evacuation order for an area more than 40 kilometres inside Lebanon, saying "the battle in Lebanon is ongoing."

items.title: Cheras karaoke centre attack linked to business feud, police nab 45 including school students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Police have reportedly said that a recent incident at a karaoke centre in Cheras, where masked men were seen storming the premises, was a planned attack linked to a business rivalry. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the group had been hired to carry out the attack, with 45 individuals aged between 14 and 57 detained, including two secondary school students, according to a report in The Star . He said the incident took place in Taman Shamelin at about 1.30pm on Sunday.

items.title: Lebanese man scours home bombed by Israel for mementos of his slain family
items.summary: ALMOST every day for the last month, Hussein Saleh has made a somber pilgrimage to the plot of land in south Lebanon where his home once stood, scouring the earth for trinkets that belonged to his wife, daughter and six other relatives killed in an Israeli strike. "Every day or two I come here, I check on things, I look around to find memories, to find a phone, to find anything that can soothe my heart and make things lighter," said Saleh, 34. "I heard two strikes and my heart sunk.

items.title: Spain: 'Unacceptable' for Israel to continue Lebanon invasion
items.summary: MADRID: Spain's foreign minister on Wednesday said it was "unacceptable" that Israel continued its invasion of Lebanon after the United States and Iran agreed a two-week ceasefire to halt the regional war. "All fronts must cease, and all fronts also means Lebanon. It is unacceptable that Israel's war, Israel's invasion of a sovereign country like Lebanon... continues," Jose Manuel Albares told public radio RNE.

items.title: More than 15 countries planning to facilitate Strait of Hormuz access: Macron
items.summary: PARIS: French President Emmanuel Macron said about 15 countries were planning to facilitate the resumption of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world's oil supply usually flows, after a ceasefire between the United States and Iran was announced. Macron said he welcomed the ceasefire between Iran and the United States, but added the situation in Lebanon remained critical and called for Lebanon to be included in the deal. France maintains close ties with Lebanon, a former protectorate.

items.title: Greece to ban social media for under 15-year-olds: PM
items.summary: ATHENS: Greece will ban access to social media for children under 15 from Jan 1, 2027 Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis said on Wednesday. "We have decided to go ahead with a difficult but necessary measure: ban access to social media for children under 15 years old," he said in a video posted on TikTok. Austria announced last month it would soon ban social media for children up to the age of 14, with a plan to present a new law "as early as this summer."

items.title: Air-conditioned factory hostel with perks conceals undocumented foreign workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Well-equipped, air-conditioned living quarters lured foreign workers to a factory here, where their stay was cut short by an Immigration Department raid during an integrated operation. The rooms were also fitted with refrigerators storing untaxed alcohol and illicit cigarettes. An Indonesian worker said he started working at the factory after coming to Malaysia on a tourist pass three years ago.

items.title: [UPDATED] Cops remand trio for disrupting MACC chief public function
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have remanded three people for taking part in a protest that disrupted a public event attended by Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Tan Sri Azam Baki yesterday. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the trio were detained following the incident and have been remanded for three days starting today to facilitate investigations. Yesterday, a public address by Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki at the Wacana Ilmiah Perdana was briefly disrupted by three youths who staged a protest during the event.

items.title: AmBank rolls out support measures amid global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AmBank Group is offering financial support to customers amid ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainties. The group said assistance may include flexible repayment plans and other tailored solutions aimed at easing short-term pressures while safeguarding long-term financial health. "Recognising that external developments may place financial strain on individuals and businesses, AmBank Group stands ready to provide support where needed.

items.title: Govt studies measures as construction costs rise
items.summary: KUCHING: The federal government is reviewing all proposals and recommendations to address rising construction material costs following tensions in Middle East, Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said. In fact, he said the ministry had met with contractor associations last week to gather their feedback on current industry conditions, particularly the surge in material prices. This issue also involves the Ministry of Finance and the Ministry of Economy," he told reporters after visiting the Construction Industry Development Board Sarawak office today.

items.title: Buntong murder: Three men remanded for two days
items.summary: Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the remand order for the suspects, aged between 24 and 34, was issued by Magistrate S. Punitha at the Magistrate’s Court here today. He said the trio were arrested at the Buntong Public Market at about 5.30pm yesterday. He said initial investigations indicate the incident stemmed from a misunderstanding, although further investigations are ongoing to determine the exact cause.

items.title: WFH policies could cost more than they save, business group says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A large-scale shift to work-from-home arrangements may generate fuel subsidy savings but risks greater economic losses across key urban sectors, Federation of Malaysian Business Associations (FMBA) said. The federation said a decline in urban consumption, particularly in the Klang Valley, could lead to monthly economic losses of between RM320 million and RM495 million, about 1.9 to 2.9 times higher than the projected savings. Based on its assessment, FMBA said petrol stations are particularly vulnerable due to their volume-driven, low-margin business model and high fixed operating costs.

items.title: Govt deploys 300 hostel warden assistants to ease teachers' workload
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 300 MyStep Hostel Warden Assistants (MyPWA) have been appointed and deployed to boarding schools nationwide from April 1 to enhance student safety and ease teachers' workload. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the MyPWA personnel will assist wardens, particularly in night-time supervision, to ensure the safety, welfare and well-being of boarding students are continuously strengthened. Fadhlina said the move is also in line with the aspirations of the Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026-2035, which emphasises the need for subject teachers to focus on teaching and learning (PdP) activities.

items.title: Part-time imam fined RM8k for recording sister-in-law bathing
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A man was fined RM8,000 by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to recording his sister-in-law while she was bathing using a mobile phone on March 24. The 36-year-old defendant entered his plea after the charge was read to him through an interpreter before Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid. Earlier, Muhammad Nadzir urged the court to impose an appropriate sentence, noting that the victim was the defendant's sister-in-law.

items.title: DBKL denies appointing third party for parking ticket collection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has denied claims that it appointed any third party, agent or subcontractor to handle parking ticket sales or manual payment collection. DBKL said all charges related to its services are made only through official channels and designated applications. "DBKL has never appointed any agent, subcontractor or third party for ticket sales or manual payment collection.

items.title: Over 50,000 expected at Perlis Majlis Kenduri Rakyat festivities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A variety of programmes and activities showcasing the uniqueness and beauty of Perlis will take centre stage at the Majlis Kenduri Rakyat in celebration of the Silver Jubilee of Raja Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Putra Jamalullail, this Monday. Perlis Menteri Besar, Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Hamzah said one of the main highlights will be the preparation of 100 large cauldrons of Kurma Daging Perlis, cooked through a community effort, involving 25 cows, alongside 100 whole roasted goats. He also said that several complementary programmes will be held throughout the month, including the Perlis Agro Fest, the Perkampungan Sunnah—which is closely associated with the Perlis government—and the Perlis International Marathon from April 24 to 26.

items.title: [UPDATED] Suspended cop, two bodyguards among six arrested over Kepong home robberies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A suspended policeman and two bodyguards are among six men arrested over a series of armed robberies targeting homes owned by foreigners in Kepong. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspects, aged between 31 and 51, were detained on April 5 following intelligence-led operations by Sentul police and the Kuala Lumpur Criminal Investigation Department. "The suspects impersonated police officers before entering the victims' homes.

items.title: Man jailed 165 years, 110 strokes for raping his two daughters in Lahad Datu
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, April 8 — A 54-year-old man was sentenced to 165 years in prison and 110 strokes of the cane after pleading guilty to raping his two biological daughters at the Sessions Court here today. Lahad Datu deputy district police chief Superintendent Jimmy Panyau said the suspect had raped his biological daughters, aged 25 and 22, at several locations in Lahad Datu and Tawau. “The suspect faced five charges involving his 25-year-old daughter and six involving his 22-year-old daughter, as well as one additional charge involving another woman.

items.title: Madani Mart not a franchise, says Fuziah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Madani Mart is not a franchise because it does not collect royalties, said Madani Foundation chairman and founder Datuk Fuziah Salleh. She added that the Madani Mart business model was prepared with a proper licensing structure, in response to allegations that it is a franchise and collects royalties. Madani Mart utilises a public-private-social partnership (PPSP) business model via licensing, where the Madani Foundation acts as the licensor for the branding, concept, and business model - all of which are registered under the Intellectual Property Corporation of Malaysia (MyIPO).

items.title: 'Total failure': Israeli opposition turns on Netanyahu over Iran truce
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's opposition leaders swiftly criticised the ceasefire with Iran on Wednesday, accusing Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of failing to achieve the war's objectives. The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in an 11th-hour bid to avert all-out destruction of Iran threatened by US President Donald Trump. Netanyahu's office said Israel supported Trump's decision to suspend the bombing of Iran, but maintained the ceasefire "does not include Lebanon" where Israeli forces are fighting Iran-backed Hizbollah.

items.title: Ten motorcyclists fined for noise pollution at Likas Public Park
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Ten motorcyclists were compounded RM300 each for emitting excessive noise during an enforcement operation at the Likas Public Park here. Sabah Department of Environment (DoE) director Nor Rashidah Ghazali said the offences were largely due to illegal modifications to motorcycle exhaust systems. Rashidah also said the DoE compounded three diesel-powered vehicles RM2,000 each for emitting black smoke.

items.title: KL police chief urges better coordination with media at crime scenes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have initiated discussions with media representatives following a misunderstanding at the scene of the recent attack on an entertainment outlet in Cheras. Gerakan Media Merdeka (Geramm) and the National Union of Journalists (NUJ) expressed concern over the alleged actions taken against three journalists from Chinese dailies who were reporting on the case. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the incident occurred as officers were securing the scene while journalists were carrying out coverage.

items.title: Malaysia receives new equipment to support Asean Observer Team at Cambodia–Thailand border
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has received specialised ceasefire monitoring equipment from Japan under its Official Security Assistance (OSA) framework to support the Asean Observer Team (AOT) at the Cambodia–Thailand border. Japan's ambassador to Malaysia, Shikata Noriyuki, said the support includes handheld radios, binoculars, laptops, printers, and large-screen monitors. "By enhancing their security and deterrence capabilities, OSA deepens our security cooperation, helps create a more favourable security environment for Japan, and contributes to maintaining and strengthening international peace and security," the Japanese ambassador said.

items.title: Kinta schools study: Too much screen time, too little outdoor activity major factors in primary school pupils' vision issues
items.summary: IPOH, April 8 —Excessive gadget use and limited outdoor activity have been identified as key factors contributing to vision problems among primary school pupils, particularly in the Kinta district. Perak Clinical Research Centre head Datuk Dr D Philip Rajan said both factors were identified through a 2023 field study involving six schools, which found that nearly 95 per cent of 667 pupils had vision issues. “The study was conducted at Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Buntong, SK Guru Kalgidhar, SK Silibin, Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Tamil (SJKT) Perak St Philomena Convent, SJKT Perak Sangeetha Sabah and Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina (SJKC) Guntong.

items.title: [UPDATED] Rivalry and personal grudges sparked violent attack on Cheras outlet, says police chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The violent attack at an entertainment outlet in Cheras on Sunday was linked to business rivalry and personal grudges involving rival groups, police said. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said more than 60 individuals were involved in the April 5 incident, which unfolded rapidly and in broad daylight. "There are also middlemen and possibly higher-level individuals involved," he said.

items.title: Unity banquet to replace cancelled Aidilfitri open house in Butterworth
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 8 — A unity banquet will be held in Bagan Ajam on April 11, replacing a cancelled Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house that was initially proposed to take place at the Thean Hock Keong Association temple grounds in Butterworth. Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Mohamad Abdul Hamid said the banquet, organised by the Zhao Zi Long Cultural and Arts Association of Penang together with Bagan Ajam residents, will be held at Dewan Panorama Bagan Ajam. “Therefore, upon the advice of the Penang State National Unity and Integration Department, the Penang State Islamic Religious Council has handed over all coordination matters for this unity banquet to the Penang State National Unity and Integration Department, as any events related to unity activities fall under the jurisdiction of this department,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Overstaying Pakistanis fined RM22,800 for immigration, licensing offences
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Two Pakistani men were fined a total of RM22,800 by the magistrate's court here today for overstaying and operating a second-hand goods business without a licence. Bakht faced an additional charge of operating a second-hand goods business without a licence, an offence under Section 6 of the Second-Hand Dealers Act 1946, punishable under Section 9 of the same Act. Bakht was also fined RM800 for the licensing offence.

items.title: SC names Manoj Kurup as executive director of enforcement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission (SC) has appointed Datuk Manoj Kurup as executive director of enforcement effective today. With over three decades of experience across the judiciary and public prosecution sector, Manoj's last held position was head of prosecution division at the Attorney-General's Chambers (AGC) before retiring in 2024. SC chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said Manoj brings with him deep legal expertise and leadership to the SC.

items.title: 288kg of packet cooking oil seized in Kedah raid
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A raid on a house in Kampung Sungai Limau, Baling, uncovered 288kg of packet cooking oil illegally stored. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry's Kedah director Muhammad Nizam Jamaludin said the operation was carried out by a team of four enforcement officers from the Baling branch at around 11am last Sunday, following a week-long intelligence-gathering exercise after receiving a public complaint. Muhammad Nizam said the case is being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961, and 288 packets of 1kg cooking oil, valued at about RM720, were seized for further investigation.

items.title: Beyond festive seasons, Malaysia’s traffic strain is already showing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — For many Malaysians, heavy traffic is often associated with festive seasons. Long weekends, school holidays, and even typical weekday commutes are now enough to put sustained pressure on highways and urban roads. Traffic slows, queues form, and flow becomes uneven.

items.title: Sabah train fare review nears completion, govt explores new Putatan-Sepanggar route
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 8 — Sabah is reviewing its railway fares and studying a potential new rail route as part of efforts to strengthen the state’s public transport network, Deputy Chief Minister III Datuk Ewon Benedick said. Ewon, who is also Sabah Industrial Development, Entrepreneurship and Transport Minister, said the Sabah State Railway Department is undertaking two key studies — a fare review and a feasibility study for a proposed line from Putatan to Kota Kinabalu, extending to the Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park (KKIP) and Sepanggar Port. Ewon added that the Sabah State Railway Department is also contributing input to the feasibility study for the Trans Borneo Railway project being undertaken by the federal government.

items.title: BUDI95 adjustments a sensible fiscal move but not without fallout risk, FitchSolutions unit says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The Malaysian government’s decision to further adjust the BUDI95 allocation would be a sensible fiscal move amid the US-Iran war, but could pose a significant political risk for Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s coalition ahead of a rumoured snap poll, according to a new report. An analysis by GeoQuant, a FitchSolutions company, on the politics of fuel subsidies in Southeast Asia said that while Malaysia’s move to adjust monthly subsidised fuel quotas from 300 to 200 litres helps control costs, prolonged high oil prices could force further unpopular measures. Conversely, “if oil prices remain elevated, needed fiscal constraint will hurt the government in state elections and stave off federal elections,” which are not due until February 2028.

items.title: Grab driver fined RM2,000 for assaulting motorcyclist in Kulai
items.summary: Yeoh Ah Chai pleaded guilty after the charge was read out by the court interpreter before Magistrate R. Salini. Based on the charge sheet, Yeoh, who is also a Grab driver, was charged with intentionally causing injury to a 37-year-old motorcyclist by punching his body at 5.45pm on April 3. Yeoh immediately alighted his vehicle and kicked the victim’s motorcycle causing it to fall.

items.title: Bar’s appeal on Zahid case moves forward, AG’s objection dismissed
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — The Court of Appeal has dismissed a preliminary objection by the attorney-general’s chambers, ruling that the Malaysian Bar’s appeal for leave to challenge the AG’s decision to secure a discharge for Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi from his corruption case will proceed. Senior federal counsel Ahmad Hanir Hambaly had raised the objection, noting that the Bar filed a separate application yesterday seeking leave to commence judicial review of the AG’s subsequent decision to take no further action in Zahid’s case. “The fact that the appellant (Bar) has filed for a judicial review of the AG’s NFA decision does not make this appeal against the High Court decision to dismiss the application for leave for the judicial review over the AG decision relating to the DNAA academic,” she said.

items.title: In JB, man fined RM7,000 for flashing his private parts in public
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — A 54-year-old man was fined RM7,000 in the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to a charge of non-physical sexual assault on a female student earlier this month. Judge Nor Aziati Jaafar ordered the accused, Ong Sheo Ming, to be jailed for seven months if he fails to settle the fine. According to the charge sheet, the accused was charged with non-physical sexual assault by exposing his private parts to a 12-year-old girl with sexual intent.

items.title: Crowds gather to bid Ling Liong Sik farewell in quiet ceremony
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — A massive crowd turned up to bid a final farewell to former MCA president Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik at Xiao En Centre in Cheras today. Flower wreaths line the floor during the wake of former transport minister Tun Ling Liong Sik at Xiao En Centre, Cheras, April 8, 2026. Among those present were several MCA leaders and veterans, including party president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong; former party presidents Tun Ong Ka Ting, Tan Sri Ting Chew Peh, Tan Sri Tan Koon Swan and Tun Lim Ah Lek; former party secretaries-general Datuk Seri Ong Ka Chuan and Datuk Seri Kong Cho Ha; former deputy president Tan Sri Chan Kong Choy; as well as former human resources minister Tan Sri Fong Chan Onn and former tourism minister Tan Sri Ng Yen Yen.

items.title: MCMC investigates 36 fake news cases on energy crisis: Strict action promised
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has opened 36 investigation papers into the spread of fake news on social and digital media related to the global energy crisis. “Six investigation papers have been referred to the deputy public prosecutor for review and further action,” it said. MCMC said it takes seriously any misuse of social and digital media platforms aimed at misleading the public through false information.

items.title: RM5m graft probe involving research funds: MACC remands four, including lecturers
items.summary: IPOH, April 8 — Four individuals, including two lecturers from a private higher education institution in northern Malaysia, have been remanded for four days to assist in an investigation into the solicitation and receipt of approximately RM5 million in bribes. Four individuals, including two lecturers from a northern private higher education institution, have been remanded for four days over an alleged RM5 million bribery case in Ipoh April 8, 2026. — Bernama pic.

items.title: ‘Just to share blessings’: Sponsor defends crying competition in Kelantan after backlash
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 8 — A sponsor of a crying competition in Kelantan has defended the event after it drew criticism online, saying participants joined voluntarily and the aim was simply to “share blessings”. Entrepreneur Wan Mohamad Rahimie Syazrin Ab Rahim said his company was the main sponsor of the three-day expo at RTC Tunjung, where the competition was held as one of several activities, according to a report in Berita Harian . The 32-year-old reportedly said the competition attracted 60 participants aged between 30 and 70, and also included a children’s category.

items.title: ‘Not favouritism’: Sabah leaders defend federal diesel subsidy, say RM2.15 rate is a necessity
items.summary: Sabah Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said difficult terrain and limited road infrastructure make diesel-powered vehicles, particularly four-wheel drives, a necessity compared to Peninsular Malaysia. Meanwhile, former Sabah Youth and Sports Minister Tan Sri Liew Yun Fah said the subsidy is not unfair to other states. The Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah (PHRS) president said the move is justified given differences in basic facilities, road conditions and higher logistics costs.

items.title: KPDN summons company over viral LPG price hike claim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has taken note of a viral message regarding adjustments to liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) prices by a company in Negeri Sembilan. In a Facebook post, KPDN said the price adjustment involved commercial gas cylinders and not subsidised domestic gas cylinders. “Nevertheless, immediate action has been taken to summon the company concerned to obtain an explanation in accordance with the Control of Supplies Act 1961 (Act 122) and the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011 (Act 723),” it said.

items.title: Form Three boy dies in three-motorcycle Terengganu crash, police link incident to ‘wheelie’ stunt
items.summary: BANDAR PERMAISURI, April 8 — A 15-year-old Form Three student reportedly died after being struck by another motorcycle while on the road following a crash in Kampung Pelong, Sungai Tong, Terengganu. According to Bernama , Setiu district police chief Superintendent Mohd Zain Mat Dris said initial investigations found the teenager had been riding a Honda C100 motorcycle and is believed to have been performing a dangerous stunt by lifting the front wheel — also known as a “wheelie”. Another motorcycle travelling in the same direction was unable to avoid him and struck him.

items.title: Anwar hails Pakistan-brokered truce between Iran and US as key to restoring global peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today welcomed the just-announced ceasefire between the United States and Iran, hailing it as a crucial step towards restoring global peace and urging that the talks lead to a lasting resolution for the entire region, including an end to the “genocide” in Palestine. Anwar specifically referenced a ten-point peace plan proposed by Iran, which he noted was “positively received by the US.” “This proposal augurs well for the restoration of peace and stability, not only to the region but also the rest of the world,” Anwar said. “Let this be a turning point towards lasting peace.

items.title: No extra BUDI95 applications: MOF denies baseless claims, says 200-litre monthly cap remains
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — Claims of additional BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) subsidy application made available for the general public are untrue and baseless, says the Ministry of Finance (MOF). In a statement yesterday, MOF said that although the function existed during the early implementation of BUDI95, it was not intended for additional applications based on personal needs. “Following this, the application button has been deactivated,” the statement said.

items.title: Police hunt Proton Saga driver over six-vehicle crash in Melaka Tengah
items.summary: MELAKA, April 8 — Police are tracking down the driver of a Proton Saga believed to have caused a six-vehicle crash along Lebuh AMJ Cheng Heights, Melaka Tengah, yesterday morning. “The Proton Saga then spun and hit several other vehicles, including a Toyota Vios, Perodua Bezza, a Nissan van and a Toyota Camry, bringing the total to six vehicles involved,” he said in a statement yesterday, adding that the driver was not at the scene and has yet to be identified. Police are tracing the driver by checking vehicle ownership, identifying witnesses and obtaining CCTV and dashcam footage.

items.title: Penang Islamic dept halts temple’s Hari Raya open house, says celebration is religious
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 8 — The Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAIPP) has ordered a Chinese temple association to cancel its planned Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house, saying the event is a Muslim religious activity that falls under the purview of the State Islamic Religious Council. “As] the celebration of Hari Raya Aidilfitri is one of the religious activities of the Muslim community, the Thean Hock Keong Association is therefore not permitted to organise an Open House Ceremony in conjunction with the said Hari Raya Aidilfitri,” the letter said. “For the information of the Thean Hock Keong Association, all Islamic religious activities including any celebration related to Hari Raya Aidilfitri are subject to the approval of the Penang State Islamic Religious Council.” Association clarifies unity-focused intent In a detailed response dated April 5, Thean Hock Keong’s secretary, Ng Choong Seong, expressed regret over the cancellation while clarifying the event’s purpose.

items.title: Police detain 12 men over Cheras entertainment centre riot where lorry was used to ram gate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Police have detained 12 men to assist in investigations into a riot and mischief at an entertainment centre in Cheras here last Sunday. Kuala Lumpur Police Chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said several vehicles believed to have been used in the incident were also seized, including a lorry that was used to ram the premises’ main gate. He said all suspects had been arrested and remand applications had been made to facilitate further investigations.

items.title: Police: Preliminary probe finds gang dispute behind Ipoh murder, not 3R issues
items.summary: IPOH, April 8 — A gang dispute is believed to have led to the murder of a man in Buntong here last night, police said. Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin said preliminary investigations found the incident involved conflict between several members of a certain group and was unrelated to racial issues. The man was killed during a fight at about 11.10pm Monday, according to Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah.

items.title: Fahmi ‘shocked’ by Papagomo posts after lawsuit settlement, seeks legal advice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The legal team for Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil is examining recent social media comments by Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as “Papagomo”, following the settlement of a lawsuit between them yesterday. Fahmi said he had received feedback from various parties regarding Wan Muhammad Azri’s latest statements and would seek legal advice to determine whether they breached the terms of the court settlement. “Yesterday, two of the three cases in this matter were settled, the first with Mohd Fauzan Madzlan, the second with Wan Muhammad Azri, and the third with Ahmad Dusuki Abd Rani.

items.title: MMEA ramps up diesel smuggling crackdown to curb subsidy leakages in national waters
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 8 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) has reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening and intensifying enforcement operations in national waters to crack down on smuggling and illegal oil transfers, including unauthorised bunkering (supplying fuel to ships) activities. MMEA director-general, Maritime Admiral Mohd Rosli Abdullah, said enforcement operations are conducted firmly and systematically, in close coordination with other agencies, to plug any gaps that could threaten national interests. “MMEA is ready to lead maritime efforts to curb leakage and smuggling of subsidised diesel, following the government’s announcement on Monday,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: MACC arrests four in Perak, including two lecturers, over RM5m bribes linked to university research funds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) arrested four individuals, including two private university lecturers, for allegedly soliciting and receiving about RM5 million in bribes. An MACC source said that the two lecturers, an oil company officer and an engineering company director were arrested at the Perak MACC office between 2pm to 5pm yesterday. The officer and the company director are believed to have acted as proxies for both the lecturers to receive illicit funds.

items.title: Perhilitan seizes RM36.8m Johor haul: Compressed tiger parts, primate parts and suspected python genitalia among contraband
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — The Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) seized a large haul of various exotic wildlife parts worth RM36.8 million at a house in Taman Johor Jaya here last Saturday. The raid netted thousands of exotic wildlife parts that are believed to be destined for the international illicit wildlife trade. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the department’s enforcement team found 602 python bile, 420 bear bile and 107 compressed pieces of various tiger parts.
